objective: Review implementation of CART-S02-T03: mostConnectedNode pure helper and carto stats CLI extension
findings: All 8 acceptance criteria met — mostConnectedNode, sentinel, bidirectional degree, tie-break, CLI extension, build, tests, lint
mostConnectedNode is a pure function in graph.ts — no I/O, no side effects, no new dependencies
CLI extension follows established pattern — chalk.cyan for most-connected, chalk.gray for advisory
chalk.gray used for (no edges) instead of planned chalk.yellow — aligns with plan-review recommendation
4 vitest tests cover sentinel, bidirectional degree, tie-break, and empty graph — all pass
ESM .js import extension used correctly in cli.ts
types.ts unchanged — return type is inline { node: Node | null; degree: number } plain object
No security concerns — pure computation on in-memory data, no persistence, no user input handling
Dangling-edge edge case: returns first 0-degree node rather than sentinel — acceptable given link() validates node existence
Test naming: tie-break test actually tests highest-degree winner; actual tie demonstrated in bidirectional degree test
npm test (25 pass), npm run build (clean), npm run lint (clean) — all verified independently
